stem: The function g is defined by g(t) = |t − 5t|. What value of c satisfies g(3) − g(c) = −20?
rationale: Choice C is correct. g(t) = |t − 5t| = |−4t| = 4|t|. So g(3) = 12 and g(c) = 4|c|. The equation g(3) − g(c) = −20 becomes 12 − 4|c| = −20, so −4|c| = −32, giving |c| = 8. Therefore c = 8 or c = −8. Of the choices given, only 8 satisfies. Choices A, B, and D do not yield a valid solution.
